facts.text: San Francisco got its name in 1847 when alcalde Washington Allon Bartlett renamed the pueblo Yerba Buena after Mission San Francisco de Asís, honoring Saint Francis of Assisi.
The city’s famous cable cars, designated a National Historic Landmark, still climb San Francisco’s steep streets today.
The Olympic Club, founded in 1860, remains the oldest athletic club in the United States.
The Fillmore, a second incarnation of a historic venue, launched the San Francisco Sound with early performances by Grateful Dead and Janis Joplin.
Bank of America built its 1969 headquarters at 555 California Street, a landmark modern skyscraper that later moved its corporate office to Charlotte.
The San Francisco Planning and Urban Research Association, founded in 1910, was created to improve housing quality after the 1906 earthquake.
